# WorldCupAI Predictor

> AI-powered World Cup 2026 match pages, score simulations, official viewing guidance, and fan prediction tools.

WorldCupAI Predictor helps fans explore World Cup 2026 fixtures with model-estimated win probabilities, predicted scores, tactical notes, and Vertex AI what-if simulations. It is an informational football analysis and entertainment site, not betting advice.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://worldcup.aiomniahub.com/)
- [All match prediction pages](https://worldcup.aiomniahub.com/matches)
- [Official viewing guide hub](https://worldcup.aiomniahub.com/watch)
- [AI score simulator](https://worldcup.aiomniahub.com/score-simulator)
- [Pricing](https://worldcup.aiomniahub.com/pricing)
- [Sitemap](https://worldcup.aiomniahub.com/sitemap.xml)

## Data And Model Notes

- Fixtures and completed scores are synced from the openfootball World Cup 2026 JSON dataset when the site sync endpoint runs.
- If external sync fails, the site falls back to a bundled local schedule snapshot.
- Win probabilities, predicted scores, confidence labels, and tactical notes are generated locally from app team ratings and AI prompts.
- Predictions are model/editorial estimates. They are not FIFA official data, sportsbook odds, betting advice, or live match telemetry.
- Viewing pages point users toward official broadcasters and licensed streaming options. The site does not provide pirated streams.

## Useful References

- Source schedule dataset: https://github.com/openfootball/worldcup.json
- Official FIFA fixtures reference: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/scores-fixtures
- Full LLM context: https://worldcup.aiomniahub.com/llms-full.txt
